ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic dumpling class (FOODON_03315671); no specific cabbage-and-pork water dumpling entry exists. Closest parent match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FKP covers wheat-based filled dumplings; no dedicated cabbage-pork shuijiao code exists. Closest parent match used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept for cabbage and pork dumplings identified in published SNOMED CT release.

serving.natural_language_portion: A standard serving of 10 boiled cabbage and pork dumplings weighs approximately 200 g, providing around 202 kcal, 17.4 g protein, and 7.6 g fat.

nutrient_source.title: Cabbage And Pork Dumplings 白菜猪肉水饺 — HPB Singapore Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Exact match found in HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id G00010912). All per_100g nutrient values sourced directly from this record with confidence 0.95.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson FS et al., American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI entry for cabbage and pork shuijiao exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 45 is estimated from the closest analogous entry: boiled wheat-flour dumplings / filled pasta (ravioli/tortellini) with meat filling, which cluster in the GI range 39–50 in Atkinson et al. 2021 and Foster-Powell et al. 2002 (Am J Clin Nutr 76:5–56). Boiling method and moderate protein/fat content suppress glycaemic response relative to plain wheat products. GL per serving calculated as: GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving / 100 = 45 × 16.06 / 100 ≈ 7.2.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium content (46 mg/100 g); potassium 82 mg/100 g
hypertension.guidance: At 46 mg sodium per 100 g, these boiled dumplings are well within a low-sodium diet; however, patients should avoid high-sodium dipping sauces such as soy sauce or chili oil, which can substantially increase total sodium intake per meal.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low estimated GI (~45), low sugar (0.21 g/100 g), moderate carbohydrate (8.03 g/100 g), good protein (8.71 g/100 g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: The low estimated GI, modest carbohydrate load, and high protein content support glycaemic control; patients should limit portion size to 6–8 pieces, avoid refined-flour wrappers in excess, pair with non-starchy vegetables, and use sugar-free dipping sauces.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate saturated fat (~1.2 g/100 g) from pork mince; cholesterol 28 mg/100 g; low sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Boiled preparation minimises added fat compared to pan-fried variants; patients with CVD should limit portion size to control saturated fat and cholesterol intake, choose lean pork mince, and avoid high-fat dipping sauces such as chili oil.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Pork is a red meat containing arachidonic acid (omega-6), which can amplify inflammatory pathways
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Patients with rheumatoid arthritis should limit red meat consumption; substituting pork with chicken, shrimp, or tofu filling reduces arachidonic acid load, while the cabbage component provides sulforaphane with modest anti-inflammatory benefit.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Cabbage and Pork Dumplings (白菜猪肉水饺, shuijiao) are a classic Chinese boiled dumpling dish widely consumed in Singapore and across the Chinese diaspora. Each dumpling consists of a thin wheat-flour wrapper encasing a seasoned filling of minced pork and finely chopped Chinese cabbage (bai cai), typically flavoured with ginger, garlic, sesame oil, and a small amount of soy sauce. The dumplings are boiled in water until the wrappers become tender and slightly translucent, yielding a soft, chewy texture with a juicy, savoury filling. Per 100 g, they provide 101 kcal, 8.71 g protein, 3.78 g fat, and 8.03 g carbohydrate, with very low sodium (46 mg) and low sugar (0.21 g), making them a nutritionally balanced option. The estimated glycaemic index is approximately 45 (low), and a 10-piece serving (approx. 200 g) delivers a glycaemic load of around 7.2. Clinically, the very low sodium content makes these dumplings suitable for hypertension management provided high-sodium dipping sauces are avoided. The low GI and moderate carbohydrate content support glycaemic control in Type 2 Diabetes. The moderate saturated fat from pork warrants portion awareness for cardiovascular disease patients. Boiling rather than frying is the preferred preparation for health-conscious consumption. Common accompaniments include black vinegar, ginger strips, and light soy sauce.
common_user_queries: 白菜猪肉水饺 热量
Can I eat dumplings with high blood pressure?

confidence_reason: Exact match found in the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id G00010912) with confidence 0.95. All core macros and secondary nutrients sourced directly from this approved primary regional source. GI is an analogous estimate from peer-reviewed literature (Atkinson et al. 2021), which reduces GI confidence to medium but does not affect overall record confidence given the strong nutrient provenance.
notes: 1. saturated_fat_g (1.20 g/100 g) is estimated from USDA FoodData Central entry for boiled pork and cabbage dumplings (FDC ID 2103689) as HPB does not report saturated fat breakdown; this is an adapted value. 2. dietary_fibre_g (0.80 g/100 g) is estimated from USDA FoodData Central closest match as HPB record does not report dietary fibre; adapted value. 3. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, vitamin panel, and moisture data for this specific HPB dumpling record are not available in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026-04-16. 4. GI of 45 is an analogous estimate based on boiled wheat-flour meat-filled dumplings from Atkinson et al. 2021 (Am J Clin Nutr) and Foster-Powell et al. 2002; no direct GI measurement for this specific product exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. 5. is_halal set to false as pork is a primary ingredient; this dish is not halal.
